rulings.Case Title: LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. DBA U.S. BANCORP VS. POPKOFF FROZEN FOODS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. DBA U.S. BANCORP'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. The Court sustains defendant's evidentiary objections 10 and 19 to the O'Toole declaration. Even if O'Toole is the custodian of records, the declaration fails to establish that the "prayer for damages worksheets" that are attached to the O'Toole declaration as Exhibits F and J, and which are the basis for her assertion that $207,302.20 is owed, are business records under Evidence Code 1270 and 1271. The motion was timely filed and served. The motion does not violate CCP section 1008, since the first motion for summary judgment was denied without prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Court Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS MARCIA GIUSTI, ANTHONY T. VLAHIOTIS DDS ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF, YANNIS VLAHOS DDS ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND AS, DONALD KINOSIAN DDS' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Court Documents The motion is denied without prejudice. The current motion does not sufficiently identify what documents, or portions thereof, should be sealed. The Court's sealing order must be narrowly tailored per CRC 2.550(d). Any renewed motion should be directed to Judge McBride, who is hearing the underlying motion for summary judgment to which this motion pertains. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion for summary judgment is denied. Moving party Mr. Contreras has not satisfied his burden of production under CCP § 437c that the "Release" applies to claims against Contreras. The Release extends to "Tropicana and Russell Inc., dba Marina Foods, their heirs, successors, insurers and assigns." Moving party has not established that this includes Mr. Contreras. Even if the burden shifted to plaintiff-in-intervention Mr. Canizales, there is a triable issue of fact whether Mr. Canizales knew at the time of the accident, and at the time Mr. Canizales signed the Release, that Contreras was an employee of Tropicana and Russell Inc., dba Marina Foods. (See evidence cited in opposition to Contreras' undisputed material fact 4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: IDA CHRISTINA FUA CRUZ VS. JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ida Fua Cruz'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 10, Plaintiff Ida Fua Cruz's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Granted. Within 10 days, Mr. Sanchez shall provide verified supplemental responses to Requests for Admission, Set Two, nos. 25 - 28. To the extent that Mr. Sanchez neither admits nor denies a specific request, Mr. Sanchez's response to that request shall comply with CCP § 2033.220(c); that is, that Mr. Sanchez has made a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter of this particular request and the information known or readily obtainable is insufficient to enable him to admit the matter. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR SHOYKHET et al VS. TODD OPPENHEIMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Defts Supplemental Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR FRIDAY, APRIL 26, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 3, PLAINTIFFS ALEKSANDR SHOYKHET & LYUDMILA SHOYKHET'S Motion To Strike DefENDANts Supplemental Expert Witnesses. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT DUNCAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE ROBERT R. DUNCAN VS. THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES, INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Further Responses To The United Council Of Human Services, Inc. And Kescha S. Mason'S Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR FRIDAY, APRIL 26, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 4, DEFENDANT THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES, INC.'S Motion Compel Further Responses To The United Council Of Human Services, Inc. And Kescha S. Mason'S Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THERE APPEARS TO BE NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION; ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION OF DEFENDANTS THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES, INC AND KESCHA S. MASON IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDER TO FILE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO UNITED COUNCIL'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SET NO. ONE, REQUESTS NOS. 1-23 AND DEFENDANT KESCHA S. MASONS REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SET NO. ONE, REQUESTS NO.3 AND 6. IF PLAINTIFF CONTINUES TO ASSERT THAT HE CAN NEITHER ADMIT NOR DENY ANY OF THESE REQUESTS HE SHOULD INSERT THE LANGUAGE SPECIFIED IN THE CODE WHEN SUCH A RESPONSE IS MADE. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO KESCHA A. MASON'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION SET NO. TWO. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $600.00 WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND COUNSEL. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 26, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: LESLEY ANN CLEMENT AS TRUSTEE FOR THE CLEMENT TRUS et al VS. TRI COLDWELL BANKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer to the second, third, tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action on standing grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Under the facts as currently pled, Plaintiff 2111-2115 Baker Street Homeowner's Association lacks standing to sue. Civ. Code section 1368.3 does not confer standing because Plaintiff has named the owner of the unit as an individual Plaintiff and Plaintiff Homeowner's Association assigned its rights to recover any legal damages resulting from construction defects to the common areas to the individual Plaintiff. (Complaint 9.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for failure to state facts sufficient is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to plead, if they can do so in good faith, the existence of a contract between Plaintiffs and this Defendant that was breached. The demurrer to the third and twelfth causes of action on statute of limitations grounds is sustained with leave to amend. As currently pled, these causes of action appear to allege wrongful conduct arising out of Defendant's Civ. Code section 2079 duty to inspect and disclose. As such, they are time-barred. (See Loken v. Century 21-Award Properties (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 263, 273-274.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead a breach or misrepresentation that does not arise from Defendant's Civ. Code section 2079 duty to inspect and disclose. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MIRIAM AVILA VS. SHIMEK PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 26, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT SHIMEK PROPERTIES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. For request no. 1, whether Plaintiff has an adequate remedy at law is a question of fact that is not proper subject of a motion to strike. However, paragraph 26(b), page 5, lines 20-24 is stricken since injunctive relief is not sought in prayer for relief. Plaintiff is given leave to add injunctive relief to the prayer for relief. For request nos. 2 - 4 regarding punitive damages, Plaintiff's allegations in support of punitive damages are conclusory, insufficient, and do not comply with Civ. Code § 3294(b). Plaintiff is given leave to amend to replead them.
